completeness_boundary: The full cited PDF was reviewed and searched. not_reported means that no explicit statement was found in that source.
audit_boundary: Expected answers were assigned after a full-text audit of all 84 PDF pages. Positive and negative answers require a method, results, or limitations passage; not_reported means that the proposition was not stated anywhere in the primary PDF, not that it is false of implementations outside the paper.

probes.question: Does Agent Laboratory itself originate the initial research idea without human input?
Does the implemented workflow autonomously generate, execute, and refine machine-learning experiment code?
Can an observed experimental result or error change a later mle-solver iteration?
Can paper-review scores cause the implemented workflow to revisit planning, experimentation, or result interpretation?
In autonomous mode, does a human intervene after supplying the initial research idea?
Is paper-solver intended to replace the human academic paper-writing process entirely?
In the reported MLE-Bench evaluation, is the mle-solver program score produced by an LLM scoring function?
Does the source explicitly identify autonomous repository-level code management as an unsupported capability of the reported implementation?
Does the source report a novelty-discrimination or prior-art validation test for the human-provided input research idea?
Does the reported workflow conduct physical, robotic, or wet-laboratory experiments?
Does the source describe persistent memory shared across separate Agent Laboratory research projects?
Does the source report a probabilistic calibration analysis of the internal mle-solver program reward model?
